name: Loop of the col d'Organbideska
region_intro: Hiking around Tardets-Sorholus, located in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department of southwestern France, offers access to the diverse landscapes of Soule, part of the Basque Country. The region is characterized by verdant terrain, with forests covering 42% of the land and meadows 30%. Elevations range from 209 to 793 meters, providing varied hiking experiences from river valleys along the Gave du Saison to the Pyrenean foothills. Best hiking trails around Tardets-Sorholus The most popular hiking route is Erretzü summit loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic 360° views of surrounding Pyrenean peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tardets – Panorama 360° loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This trail provides extensive vistas across the landscape, including the Madeleine Hill. Local hikers also love the La Madeleine – La Madelaine Chapel loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the historic chapel, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Hiking around Tardets-Sorholus is defined by verdant terrain, dense forests, and scenic Pyrenean foothills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from accessible loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Tardets-Sorholus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Tardets-Sorholus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Tardets-Sorholus?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Tardets-Sorholus, offering a variety of experiences. These trails range from easy loops to more challenging ascents into the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Tardets-Sorholus?
A: The region around Tardets-Sorholus features diverse terrain, characterized by verdant landscapes, dense forests, and expansive meadows. You'll find trails along river valleys, such as the Gave du Saison, and ascents into the scenic Pyrenean foothills, with elevations ranging from 209 to 793 meters.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Tardets-Sorholus?
A: Yes, there are several easier options suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Madeleine – La Madelaine Chapel loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.8 km trail that can be completed in about 2 hours, offering varied terrain around the historic chapel.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see while hiking?
A: Many trails offer access to significant natural features and landmarks. You can enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/797632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorama 360°&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the historic Madeleine Hill and Chapel. The region is also close to the stunning Gorges de Kakuetta and the famous Passerelle d'Holzarte (Holzarte footbridge), which are popular attractions for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Tardets-Sorholus are circular. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/993216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erretzü summit loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8 km route offering panoramic 360° views of peaks like Pierre Saint Martin and Pic d'Orhy.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tardets-Sorholus?
A: The region is excellent for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer provides longer daylight hours for more extensive explorations. Even winter can be suitable for lower-elevation walks, though higher Pyrenean trails may require specific gear.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Tardets-Sorholus?
A: The hiking trails in Tardets-Sorholus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning panoramic views from summits like Erretzü, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any trails that offer extensive panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets – Panorama 360° loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.8 km path specifically known for its extensive vistas across the landscape, including the Madeleine Hill. Another great option for views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41477893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erretzü summit – At the summit of Erretzu loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many popular starting points, such as Chapelle de la Madeleine, typically offer designated parking areas for hikers. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails using public transport?
A: Public transport options in the immediate vicinity of trailheads can be limited. Tardets-Sorholus itself is a small town, and while there might be regional bus services, accessing specific, more remote trail starting points often requires a car. For detailed information on local transport, you might consult the &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme64.com/en/our-destinations/welcome-to-the-basque-country/tardets-sorholus/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;local tourism office website&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: While the guide data indicates most routes are moderate, the region's varied elevations and Pyrenean foothills offer opportunities for more strenuous hikes. Routes with significant elevation gain, such as those leading to the Erretzü summit, provide a good challenge with rewarding panoramic views.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Tardets-Sorholus?
A: Hiking durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Shorter loops, like the La Madeleine trail, can take around 2 hours, while longer panoramic routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets-Sorholus Church loop from Tardets-Sorholus&lt;/a&gt; (11.1 km), might take 3.5 to 4 hours to complete.
tips:
Located in the heart of the Iraty Forest, the Bagargui Pass is reached via a beautiful 28-kilometer climb from Saint-Jean-le-Vieux. The climb averages 4%, with peaks reaching 11%. 
At the pass, you'll find the famous Iraty chalets and a grocery store for supplies.
